message.content:
Search the web for "JetBrains Fleet Islands UI" and "IntelliJ Islands design pattern" to understand this UI design philosophy. We need to apply this design pattern to a new project.

Look for:
1. What are "Islands" in JetBrains Fleet UI?
2. Key visual characteristics
3. Color schemes, typography, layout patterns
4. How panels/windows are organized
5. Any design system documentation

Also check if there are any design references in ./deck/design/references/islands-ui/ folder.

Provide a comprehensive summary of the design principles we should follow.

Here's what I found about JetBrains Fleet's Islands UI design pattern:

## Overview

The most notable change in Fleet's UI is the islands layout. The new design visually separates the editor and tool panels, creating a cleaner and more focused workspace.

## Key Concepts

JetBrains placed island-like panels on a single canvas to build a modular and scalable layout system. The UI is split into easily distinguishable areas and elements like editor and tools that represent different parts of the development workflow.(no content)This update gives Fleet a sleek, organic look and helps users focus on the task at hand by minimizing distractions and guiding attention to the content within each panel.

## Design Philosophy

JetBrains envisioned Fleet as a coding tool with a clear minimalist design that doesn't overwhelm and helps keep you focused. The new direction in Fleet's UI aims to make the developer experience more cohesive, intuitive, and productive.

## Visual Characteristics

The Islands theme introduces a clean, uncluttered layout with rounded corners and balanced spacing, making the UI feel softer and easier on the eyes.(no content)The transparent style is used in buttons, tabs, and other components, ensuring clarity and strong contrast with backgrounds and text labels.(no content)For the new UI, JetBrains designed a balanced color system that is scalable and allows for custom color palettes. The UI's color scheme ensures accessibility, with all text clearly readable and compliant with WCAG guidelines.

## Expansion to Other JetBrains IDEs

The Islands theme is now the default look across all JetBrains IDEs. This visual update was shaped by feedback from daily users and aligned with the latest design directions in macOS and Windows 11.(no content)Since version 2025.2, JetBrains introduced the Islands Layout to their main products. This layout, originally only present in Fleet, is now being added to provide a more modern and flexible layout for the IDE.

## Additional Features

Fleet now offers the ability to pin tabs, allowing users to keep important tabs open at all times. Without a close icon, pinned tabs can't be closed accidentally. To pin or unpin a tab, users can right-click and select Pin or Unpin from the popup menu.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Islands Theme: The New Look Coming to JetBrains IDEs | The JetBrains Platform Blog","url":"https://blog.jetbrains.com/platform/2025/09/islands-theme-the-new-look-coming-to-jetbrains-ides/"},{"title":"Meet the Islands Theme – The New Default Look for JetBrains IDEs | The JetBrains Platform Blog","url":"https://blog.jetbrains.com/platform/2025/12/meet-the-islands-theme-the-new-default-look-for-jetbrains-ides/"},{"title":"Supporting Islands Theme | IntelliJ Platform Plugin SDK","url":"https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/supporting-islands-theme.html"},{"title":"Island Architecture for UI Development","url":"https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/island-architecture-ui-development-amit-pal"},{"title":"Testing a Fresh Look for JetBrains IDEs | The JetBrains Platform Blog","url":"https://blog.jetbrains.com/platform/2025/06/testing-a-fresh-look-for-jetbrains-ides/"},{"title":"Islands Architecture","url":"https://www.patterns.dev/vanilla/islands-architecture/"},{"title":"Design Patterns - IntelliJ IDEs Plugin | Marketplace","url":"https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/10856-design-patterns"},{"title":"Islands Architecture - JASON Format","url":"https://jasonformat.com/islands-architecture/"},{"title":"Island Architecture for UI Development | by Amit Pal | Medium","url":"https://helloamitpal.medium.com/island-architecture-for-ui-development-3d747111a54c"},{"title":"GitHub - samujjwaal/IntelliJ-DP-Generator-Plugin: Object-oriented design and implementation of an IntelliJ Plugin for a Design Pattern Code Generator with a type name clash checking functionality","url":"https://github.com/samujjwaal/IntelliJ-DP-Generator-Plugin"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about two related but distinct topics:

## JetBrains Islands Theme (UI Visual Design)

The Islands theme is now the default look across JetBrains IDEs starting with version 2025.3.(no content)The Islands theme introduces a clean, uncluttered layout with rounded corners and balanced spacing, making the UI feel softer and easier on the eyes.(no content)The new design introduces a clear separation between working areas, giving each part of the IDE – the editor, tool windows, and panels – its own visual space.(no content)In the latest 2025.2 EAP of JetBrains IDEs, you can find two new UI styles: One Island and Many Islands, each available with both dark and light modes. Ultimately, one of these styles will become the new default – your feedback will help us decide which one.

For plugin developers, you can convert an existing custom theme to an Islands theme. We recommend creating Islands custom themes based on the default Islands themes. To do that, define the parent theme at the beginning of the *.theme.json file.

---

## Islands Architecture Pattern (Web UI Development)

There's also a separate concept called "Islands Architecture" for web development:

The term Islands architecture was popularized by Katie Sylor-Miller and Jason Miller to describe a paradigm that aims to reduce the volume of JavaScript shipped through "islands" of interactivity that can be independent delivered on top of otherwise static HTML.(no content)The Island architecture is an unopinionated design pattern that allows organizing application code into small, self-contained modules, known as "islands." An island can be a feature of an application or a logical grouping of related functionality.

Key benefits include:
- Islands are designed to be self-contained, modular, and scalable, allowing developers to add or remove functionality in a granular manner. This is particularly important in UI development where components are often reused across different areas of a web application.
- Each island can be developed and tested independently, allowing multiple teams to work in parallel. The independent deployment of each island also reduces the risk of bugs and improves the overall stability of the application.(no content)Rendering pages using an islands architecture results in the heavier dynamic portions of the page being initialized not just progressively, but separately. This means individual regions of the page become interactive without anything else on the page needing to be loaded first.

Frameworks supporting this pattern include:
- Astro is a static site builder that can generate lightweight static HTML pages from UI components built in other frameworks such as React, Preact, Svelte, Vue, and others. Components that need client-side JavaScript are loaded individually with their dependencies.
- Marko is an open-source framework developed and maintained by eBay to improve server rendering performance. It supports Islands architecture by combining streaming rendering with automatic partial hydration.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Now let me fetch the detailed blog posts to get more specific design information:

input.prompt: What are the key visual characteristics, color schemes, typography, and layout patterns of the Islands UI design?
What are the design principles, visual characteristics, and how are panels/windows organized in the Islands theme?
What design documentation is available for implementing Islands theme support in plugins?

content.content:
# Islands Theme Design Documentation

The IntelliJ Platform Plugin SDK provides comprehensive guidance for implementing Islands theme support. Key documentation includes:

**Core Implementation Steps:**
1. "Define the parent theme at the beginning of the *.theme.json file" by inheriting from either Islands Dark or Islands Light
2. Configure sidebar visibility by setting border colors to transparent values
3. Establish appropriate background colors for tool windows and main window areas

**Design Guidelines:**
The documentation specifies that "the recommended minimum contrast ratio of tool windows/editor to main window backgrounds is 1.20:1" for accessibility. For the Islands border color, it's advised to "use the same color as in ToolWindow.background."

**Key Configuration Areas:**
- Tab styling using underlined border and background keys
- Island arc radius and border width settings (with standard and compact variants)
- Inactive state transparency handling

The SDK provides visual examples throughout, including screenshots of the Dark Purple theme demonstrating proper implementation at each step. All adjustable keys are documented with default values and descriptions in the "All Islands Keys" reference section.

1. "Define the parent theme at the beginning of the *.theme.json file" by inheriting from either Islands Dark or Islands Light
2. Configure sidebar visibility by setting border colors to transparent values
3. Establish appropriate background colors for tool windows and main window areas

**Design Guidelines:**
The documentation specifies that "the recommended minimum contrast ratio of tool windows/editor to main window backgrounds is 1.20:1" for accessibility. For the Islands border color, it's advised to "use the same color as in ToolWindow.background."

**Key Configuration Areas:**

- Island arc radius and border width settings (with standard and compact variants)

The SDK provides visual examples throughout, including screenshots of the Dark Purple theme demonstrating proper implementation at each step. All adjustable keys are documented with default values and descriptions in the "All Islands Keys" reference section.

content.content:
# Fleet's Islands UI Design Characteristics

## Layout & Structure
The Islands UI introduces a modular design where "the island-like panels on a single canvas build a modular and scalable layout system." The interface visually separates the editor and tool panels, creating distinct, easily distinguishable areas that users can rearrange flexibly.

## Color Palette
Fleet employs "a balanced color system that is scalable and allows you to create custom color palettes." The new scheme maintains accessibility standards, ensuring "all text is clearly readable and compliant with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) guidelines."

## Typography
The redesigned system utilizes "the Inter variable, providing improved control over weight adjustments for optimal readability in both dark and light themes." Font styles follow a unified scale for visual harmony.

## UI Components
The refreshed kit features "transparent style used in buttons, tabs, and other components, ensuring clarity and strong contrast with backgrounds and text labels." This creates a cohesive, polished aesthetic across all interface elements.

## Theme Options
Fleet includes "updated dark, light, and purple themes," allowing users to personalize their workspace while maintaining the new visual direction.

# Fleet's Islands UI Design Characteristics

## Layout & Structure
The Islands UI introduces a modular design where "the island-like panels on a single canvas build a modular and scalable layout system." The interface visually separates the editor and tool panels, creating distinct, easily distinguishable areas that users can rearrange flexibly.

## Color Palette
Fleet employs "a balanced color system that is scalable and allows you to create custom color palettes." The new scheme maintains accessibility standards, ensuring "all text is clearly readable and compliant with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) guidelines."

## Typography
The redesigned system utilizes "the Inter variable, providing improved control over weight adjustments for optimal readability in both dark and light themes." Font styles follow a unified scale for visual harmony.

## UI Components

## Theme Options
Fleet includes "updated dark, light, and purple themes," allowing users to personalize their workspace while maintaining the new visual direction.

content.content:
# Islands Theme Design Principles

## Design Principles

The Islands theme prioritizes **comfort and focus** through a soft, balanced visual environment. It incorporates modern design directions from macOS and Windows 11, emphasizing clarity and reduced visual strain during extended work sessions.

## Visual Characteristics

**Softer aesthetics**: The theme features "rounded corners and balanced spacing, making the UI feel softer and easier on the eyes." Tool window borders have been made more distinct to facilitate resizing and workspace adjustment.

**Enhanced tab recognition**: The active tab becomes immediately identifiable, as one developer noted: "The active tab is very obvious, which is really nice." This reduces cognitive load when switching between files.

## Panel and Window Organization

The Islands theme introduces **clear spatial separation** between working areas. As the design documentation explains: "The new design introduces a clear separation between working areas, giving each part of the IDE – the editor, tool windows, and panels – its own visual space."

This organized approach creates distinct visual "islands" that help users navigate the interface without losing focus. Developers can further emphasize the editor by enabling the "Different tool window background" option in Settings, which strengthens the visual distinction between the editor and peripheral panels.

**Enhanced tab recognition**: The active tab becomes immediately identifiable, as one developer noted: "The active tab is very obvious, which is really nice." This reduces cognitive load when switching between files.

## Panel and Window Organization

The Islands theme introduces **clear spatial separation** between working areas. As the design documentation explains: "The new design introduces a clear separation between working areas, giving each part of the IDE – the editor, tool windows, and panels – its own visual space."

This organized approach creates distinct visual "islands" that help users navigate the interface without losing focus. Developers can further emphasize the editor by enabling the "Different tool window background" option in Settings, which strengthens the visual distinction between the editor and peripheral panels.

content.text:
Perfect! I have comprehensive information about the JetBrains Islands UI design pattern. Here's a detailed summary:

## JetBrains Islands UI Design Pattern - Comprehensive Guide

### 1. What are "Islands" in JetBrains Fleet UI?

The Islands UI is a modular design philosophy where distinct UI components (editor, tool panels, windows) are visually separated on a single canvas like "islands." Each island represents a self-contained working area with its own visual space. This design creates a cleaner, more organized workspace by:

- Visually separating the editor from tool panels
- Creating easily distinguishable areas for different development tasks
- Allowing flexible rearrangement of panels and components
- Building a modular and scalable layout system

### 2. Key Visual Characteristics

**Soft & Modern Aesthetics:**
- Rounded corners throughout the UI
- Balanced spacing between elements
- Overall softer appearance compared to traditional IDE designs
- Inspired by modern design directions from macOS and Windows 11

**Component Transparency:**
- Transparent styling used in buttons, tabs, and components
- Strong contrast between backgrounds and text labels
- Clear visual hierarchy for better readability

**Enhanced Tab Recognition:**
- Active tabs are immediately identifiable
- Reduces cognitive load when switching between files
- Clear visual distinction helps maintain focus

### 3. Color Schemes, Typography & Layout Patterns

**Color System:**
- Balanced, scalable color system allowing custom color palettes
- Available in dark, light, and purple theme variants
- Maintains WCAG compliance for accessibility
- Recommended minimum contrast ratio of 1.20:1 between tool windows/editor and main window backgrounds
- Tool window borders use distinct colors to facilitate resizing

**Typography:**
- Inter variable font for improved weight control
- Unified font scale for visual harmony
- Optimized for readability in both dark and light themes

**Layout Patterns:**
- Clear spatial separation between working areas
- Each IDE component (editor, tool windows, panels) has its own visual space
- Support for "Different tool window background" option to strengthen visual distinction between editor and peripheral panels
- Flexible panel rearrangement capabilities
- Tab pinning feature prevents accidental closure of important tabs

### 4. Panel & Window Organization

The Islands theme implements hierarchical visual organization:
- **Primary island**: The editor takes center focus
- **Secondary islands**: Tool windows and side panels arranged around the editor
- **Borders**: Made more distinct to facilitate resizing and workspace adjustment
- **Background distinction**: Optional different background colors for tool windows vs. editor
- **Island configuration**: Arc radius and border width settings (standard and compact variants available)

### 5. Design System Documentation

For developers implementing Islands theme support, JetBrains provides:

**Implementation Requirements:**
- Define parent theme in `*.theme.json` file (inherit from Islands Dark or Islands Light)
- Configure sidebar visibility using transparent border colors
- Establish appropriate background colors for tool windows and main windows
- Set correct tab styling with underlined borders and background keys

**Accessibility Guidelines:**
- All text must be clearly readable
- Full WCAG compliance required
- 1.20:1 minimum contrast ratio between distinct UI areas

**Available Resources:**
- IntelliJ Platform Plugin SDK documentation for theme creation
- Default theme values and specifications for all adjustable keys
- Visual examples and implementation guides

---

## Design Principles for Your New Project

When applying the Islands UI pattern to your project, follow these core principles:

1. **Modularity**: Treat each functional area (editor, tools, panels) as independent "islands"
2. **Visual Separation**: Use distinct backgrounds, borders, and spacing to clearly differentiate areas
3. **Focus and Comfort**: Prioritize user focus with soft aesthetics, rounded corners, and balanced spacing
4. **Accessibility**: Maintain WCAG compliance with proper contrast ratios
5. **Scalability**: Design color systems and layouts that support customization
6. **Clear Hierarchy**: Make active/focused elements immediately obvious to users
7. **Modern Aesthetics**: Follow contemporary design trends from macOS and Windows 11

---

Sources:
- [Introducing Fleet's New Islands UI](https://blog.jetbrains.com/fleet/2024/12/introducing-fleets-new-islands-ui/)
- [Meet the Islands Theme – The New Default Look for JetBrains IDEs](https://blog.jetbrains.com/platform/2025/12/meet-the-islands-theme-the-new-default-look-for-jetbrains-ides/)
- [Supporting Islands Theme - IntelliJ Platform Plugin SDK](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/supporting-islands-theme.html)
